verdict.answer_first: A leads with the command: `git reset --soft HEAD~1`; B starts with `Use a soft reset` then gives the command.
verdict.decisiveness: A gives one clear prescription and only a brief caveat; B expands into `three reset modes`, `commit --amend`, and `force-with-lease`.
verdict.fabrication: none; B adds unasked but valid context: `If the commit was already pushed... force-push`.
verdict.actionability: A is faster to act on because the first line is the exact command and the explanation stays scoped to staged changes.
